Shishir Agrawal

Basic information

  • Email: @
  • Website: math.berkeley.edu/~sagrawal/
  • Citizenship: USA

Education

  • PhD in mathematics (expected May 2018). UC Berkeley. GPA: 4.00.
  • BS in mathematics, summa cum laude (2011). UC San Diego. GPA: 3.96.
    • Minors in computer science and general biology.

Computer skills

  • Programming: Java, C, Haskell
  • Web design: HTML5, CSS, Javascript, PHP
  • Other: Linux, Microsoft Office, LaTeX, Inkscape
  • CS-related coursework: algorithms, programming languages, theory of computation, graph theory

Experience

  • Graduate student researcher. UC Berkeley (2011-present).
    • Conducted independent and supervised research in arithmetic geometry.
    • Awarded the Chateaubriand fellowship to conduct research in France.
    • Authored the paper "Cohomological rigidity via deformation theory" (arXiv:1710.03404).
  • Graduate student instructor. UC Berkeley (2011-present).
    • Designed and taught three math courses as the primary instructor.
    • Collaborated with other instructors to teach undergraduate math courses as a teaching assistant.
    • Mentored talented undergraduates to help them learn advanced mathematics.
  • Undergraduate researcher. UC San Diego (2011).
    • Conducted supervised research in algebraic geometry for an undergraduate thesis.
    • Awarded honors with highest distinction.
  • Teaching assistant, Grader. UC San Diego (2009-2011).
    • Taught discussion sections for calculus courses.
    • Graded for honors calculus courses.
  • Undergraduate researcher. Institute for Pure and Applied Mathematics, UC Los Angeles (2009).
    • Collaborated with a team of four students to design and implement an expectation-maximization algorithm for calibrating for errors in mass spectrometry data.
  • Undergraduate researcher. Trey Ideker Lab, UC San Diego (2008-2009).
    • Collaborated with a team to develop computational models of the genetic regulation of plant circadian clocks.
    • Awarded the Chancellor's research scholarship.
  • Summer intern. Symyx Technologies (2008).
    • Served as a software test engineering intern.

Languages

  • Fluent: English, Hindi
  • Intermediate: French, Japanese

Honors and awards

  • Chateaubriand fellowship at the University of Strasbourg (2016-2017). Embassy of France.
  • Summer grant (2012, 2013, 2016). UC Berkeley.
  • Graduate student researcher (2015). FRG: Collaborative Research: In and Around Theory X.
  • Muir scholar (2011). John Muir College, UC San Diego.
  • Honors with highest distinction (2011). Department of Mathematics, UC San Diego.
  • Dean's award for excellence (2009). Division of Physical Sciences, UC San Diego.
  • Chancellor's research scholarship (2009). UC San Diego.
  • Regents scholarship (2007-2011). UC San Diego.
